title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has released a document outlining its response to a report on artificial intelligence and public standards, likely committing to new policies and regulations in this area. This document aims to outline how the government will address the recommendations made in the 2020 report to ensure public safety and trust in AI systems.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This document assesses the impact of using copyrighted material in the development of AI systems, examining the potential consequences and copyright implications. It provides a report on the intersection of copyright and AI to guide future development and use of AI technology.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This document outlines the Department for Education's policy on the use of generative artificial intelligence (AI) in schools and education institutions. It aims to clarify expectations and guidelines for the safe and responsible use of generative AI in the education sector.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport Artificial Intelligence Action Plan aims to harness the benefits of AI in the transport sector. It outlines how the Department for Transport (DfT) will collaborate with the industry to capitalize on AI opportunities while addressing potential risks.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document provides an update on the use of copyrighted materials in creating AI systems, specifically as mandated under a section of the Data (Use and Access) Act. It presents a report on the progress made regarding this matter.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The UK government is responding to recommendations on regulating artificial intelligence used in medical devices. This response aims to provide a framework for the safe and effective use of AI in medical treatments.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual is creating a policy for regulating AI in the qualifications sector at the request of the Education and Science Secretaries. The policy aims to ensure AI is used fairly and safely in exams and qualifications.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government responded to the House of Lords Select Committee's report on artificial intelligence, titled 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'. The response likely outlines the government's plans and actions in addressing the concerns and recommendations made by the committee to improve the country's AI landscape.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference for a review of artificial intelligence and its impact on public standards. It aims to guide a committee in its exploration of the intersection between AI and public standards, providing a foundation for its inquiry and any subsequent recommendations.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has provided evidence to a government inquiry on how to effectively govern Artificial Intelligence. The submission aims to inform the UK's approach to governing AI to ensure its development and use benefit society.
